claims.claim:
Abstract
Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) have recently gained
widespread attention as a successful tool for analyzing graph-
structured data.

However, imperfect graph structure with
noisy links lacks enough robustness and may damage graph
representations, therefore limiting the GNNs’ performance in
practical tasks.

Moreover, existing generative architectures
fail to fit discriminative graph-related tasks.

To tackle these
issues, we introduce an unsupervised method based on a joint
of generative training and discriminative training to learn
graph structure and representation, aiming to improve the
discriminative performance of generative models.

We pro-
pose an Energy-based Contrastive Learning (ECL) guided
Graph Structure Refinement (GSR) framework, denoted as
ECL-GSR.

To our knowledge, this is the first work to com-
bine energy-based models with contrastive learning for GSR.

Specifically, we leverage ECL to approximate the joint dis-
tribution of sample pairs, which increases the similarity be-
tween representations of positive pairs while reducing the
similarity between negative ones.

Refined structure is pro-
duced by augmenting and removing edges according to the
similarity metrics among node representations.
